title: Intensely Crispy Syrian Chicken Kebabs
description: A yeasty, crispy Syrian dish featuring chicken kebabs cooked in a claypot with vegetable shortening, perfect for a sick day. This double-fry method creates an intensely crispy exterior and a juicy interior.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together yeast, garlic powder, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Add the chicken pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the yeast mixture.
Let the chicken marinate for 30 minutes to allow the yeast to activate.
Heat 1/4 cup of vegetable shortening in a claypot over medium-high heat to 350 degrees.
Add the chicken pieces to the claypot in batches and fry until golden brown, about 5 minutes per batch.
Remove the chicken from the claypot and let it cool on a paper towel-lined plate for 5 minutes.
Increase the heat to high and add the remaining 1/4 cup of vegetable shortening to the claypot.
Add the chicken pieces back to the claypot in batches and fry until intensely crispy, about 2-3 minutes per batch.
Remove the chicken from the claypot and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Drizzle the lemon juice over the chicken kebabs and serve hot.
